# Clerk Docs Markdown

An offline-first, LLM-optimized collection of the entire Clerk API documentation. Mirrors the official Clerk Docs in clean, structured Markdown, perfectly formatted for RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) and AI context.

## Navigation

- [README.md](./README.md) - Project overview and features.
- [structure.md](./structure.md) - Full hierarchy and file list of the documentation (700+ docs).

## Documentation Categories

All documentation is located in the `docs/` directory.

- [Getting Started](./docs/getting-started/) - Quickstarts for Next.js, React, Astro, Vue, Nuxt, Remix, React Router, Expo, Express, Fastify, Go, Ruby, and more.
- [Guides](./docs/guides/) - Core concepts, customizing components, auth flows, organizations, billing, user management, secure settings, webhooks, and troubleshooting.
- [Reference](./docs/reference/) - Detailed SDK guides, React Component documentation, Custom Hooks, Backend API (BAPI), Frontend API (FAPI), and Integrations.

## Developer Guide for LLMs

When using this documentation to write code, solve bugs, analyse projects, or plan integrations, follow these protocols:

### 1. Planning & Exploration
- **Start with Quickstarts**: If you are starting a new project or adding Clerk to an existing one, always read the specific quickstart for your framework in `docs/getting-started/`. They provide the exact `<ClerkProvider>` setup and middleware logic.
- **Understand Core Concepts**: Review `docs/getting-started/core-concepts.md` to learn about Sessions, Users, and Organizations.

### 2. Implementation & Coding
- **Components vs. Hooks**: Use the pre-built components (e.g., `<SignIn />`, `<UserButton />`) documented in `docs/reference/components/` first. Only drop down to custom React hooks (e.g., `useAuth()`, `useUser()`) documented in `docs/reference/hooks/` if you need a fully custom UI.
- **Middleware**: Middleware configuration determines which routes are public or protected. Check the framework-specific middleware docs (like `docs/reference/nextjs/clerk-middleware.md`).
- **Backend SDK**: If you need to mutate users/organizations from your server-side code, use the JS Backend SDK `clerkClient`. See `docs/reference/backend/`.

### 3. Debugging & Troubleshooting
- **Errors**: Consult `docs/guides/development/errors/` for specific Backend and Frontend API errors.
- **Webhooks**: Check `docs/guides/development/webhooks/` if you are syncing Clerk data (like user creation) to your own database.

Note: These docs were converted from the open-source Clerk documentation repository. MDX components (Tabs, Accordions, callouts) have been converted to standard Markdown for ease of reading.
